sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 9.45 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.9)

statement.latex: Let$a$be a vector in${ R}^n$with rational coordinates and set$$S(a)=\{ ka + b k { Z},\,\,\, b { Z}^n\} .$$-1ex It is obvious that$S(a)$is a discrete subgroup in${ R}^n$of rank$n$. Find necessary and sufficient conditions in terms of the coordinates of$a$for$S(a)$to have an orthogonal basis with respect to the standard scalar product in${ R}^n$.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
